mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.8% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5% over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where inventory buildup coincides with weakening demand, forecasting negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power for forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or fails to outperform a baseline INDPRO-only signal in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes, a persistent rise in core inflation erodes real yields and compresses equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not negatively correlated with the combined signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly expansion in real GDP growth above the historical median reflects robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate a positive mean forward return exceeding 1.5% over the 60-day window, or the Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts fall below 1.4 million units annually, it signals a sharp pullback in business and consumer confidence, triggering a risk-off environment that depresses equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline market momentum factor.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where employment growth accelerates while industrial production stagnates indicates a labor-intensive service economy shift that strains corporate margins, forecasting negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing, or the combined signal produces a positive mean forward return exceeding 2% over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, sustained growth in real consumer credit balances fuels demand-pull inflation that forces prolonged restrictive monetary policy, ultimately contracting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive relationship reverses direction during periods of disinflation.
